scope: Inventory of local research and dataset assets

researches.title: Hybrid Quantum-Classical Ensemble Learning for S&P 500 Directional Prediction
weaknesses_flagged: Adaptive overfitting risk (35 model combinations selected)
No transaction-cost-aware optimization beyond toy example
Quantum feature on classical simulator — no real quantum advantage demonstrated
No out-of-sample walk-forward; single temporal 70/30 split
researches.reproducibility_risk: Medium (no public code provided as of paper)
useful_for_design: Quantifies gain from confidence-based filtering (smart filter ≥52%)
Demonstrates SOTA-realistic ceiling ≈60% directional accuracy for S&P 500 daily
Confirms regime-dependency: best in moderate-to-high VIX; degrades at VIX>35

researches.title: Improving Cryptocurrency Price Direction Forecasting: Confidence Score Thresholds on Random Forest Ensembles
researches.target: binary direction at t + 60 bars (5h on 5-min bars; 60m on 1-min bars)
researches.method: Rolling-window retraining, N=2016 / K=60, threshold T on summed confidence
weaknesses_flagged: Accuracy is conditional on the threshold T — high accuracy with very small coverage is not the same as high predictive skill
Threshold selection introduces selection bias / overfitting on a single backtest
RF1+RF2 same architecture — correlation >0.6 likely; limited diversity
Only 2 horizons tested (60 bars), no multi-horizon
No live trading validation; no realistic slippage beyond price-impact acknowledgement
useful_for_design: Confidence-threshold trade-off is explicit and useful for product UX
Rolling retraining cadence (7-day window, 5h test) is a sensible default
Adaptive Markets Hypothesis is the right framing for crypto (less mature, less efficient)
Suggests 60-75% accuracy is the realistic ceiling on high-frequency crypto with conf filtering
Provides concrete indicator list (43 candidates, ~10 used)

llm_usage_in_local_papers: None of the local papers uses LLMs; this is a clear gap relative to the user requirement.
gaps_detected: No LLM/RAG architecture in local papers — we must add an LLM (Ollama cloud) layer for research synthesis, hypothesis generation, evidence-grounded explanation, and user-facing reasoning.
No explicit purged/walk-forward evaluation in either paper — we must enforce this from day one.
No realistic slippage / market-impact model in either paper — we must add it.
No stocks dataset in local assets — we need to source one (e.g., TWRR).
No on-chain live data pipeline described — we need to decide how to use CryptoQuant/Glassnode.
No UX/persona work in either paper — we must design this from scratch.
